Summary
As a developer, I would like to develop a feature in the new CRM that would enable a user to create a Purchase Request. A sales team member creates a Purchase Request to initiate the procurement process. Each Purchase Request represents a single part, i.e., a request line item. Multiple purchase requests should be created for multi-part requests.
Acceptance Criteria
After completion of the task, a user should be able to create a purchase order for a part in the new CRM
Architecture Guidance
Functional use cases - here
Functional Tasks 
Sourcing Request - Create Sourcing request from Sales Request (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-2913)
Sourcing Request - Details Overview (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-2914)
Sourcing Request - Connect new sales request with an Existing Sourcing Request (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-2916)
Sourcing Request - Create a task to indicate a Sourcing request is assigned to a Buyer (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-2924)
Datamodel -   
Figma design - here
A new set of APIs should be developed in the chip1-transaction service as follows 



Use case
Endpoint
Comments
Purchase Request creation
[POST] /purchase-request
Create a purchase request from a RFQ line item. The line details should be copied in the purchase request
A task shall be created automatically once the purchase request is created. Here is the task API Sourcing Request - Create a task to indicate a Sourcing request is assigned to a Buyer (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-2924)
The data should be saved in the MongoDB and indexed in the OpenSearch.
Figma Designs - design1, design2
Purchase Request update
[PATCH] /purchase-request/{id}
Link a RFQ line item with an existing purchase request
Editing an existing purchase request
The update should be reflected in the OpenSearch too.
Figma design - here
Purchase Request list view
[GET] /puchase-request/
Get a list of purchase request
Filter by rfq id & rfq line id
Figma design - here
Purchase Request get by ID
[GET] /puchase-request/{id}
Get purchase request details by ID
HERO section details
Figma design - here
RFQ List view
[GET] /rfq
RFQ filter by purchase request
Entity Events
The following events should be generated during create/update operations - 
Timeline event
It helps to keep track of the audit details of the entity. The event shall be raised using the chip1-common library.
Trigger point - Create & Update of an entity
Databits event
It keeps track of the entity count in different contexts, i.e., accounts, contacts, parts. The event shall be raised using the chip1-common library.
Trigger point - Creation of an entity
Part-activity event
This event keeps track of the activity of parts.
Trigger point - Creation of an entity
 

Connect Service changes
The Sourcing Request data sync shall not happen with the xCRM app. We will evaluate it later.
Notes: The purchase request sync shall happen after the sales order is created. But we need more details about the xCRM behaviour.
API Guidance

Acceptance Criteria
 Navigation & Entry Point
From the Left Navigation, under Sales, select Requests.
This opens a two-tabbed view:
Requests tab
Request Lines tab
Requests Tab Overview
Each Request is a consolidated record that groups multiple Request Lines.
The hero section at the top summarizes key details:
Total # of Parts
Total # of Units 
Total Targeted Price
Total Value
Status Summary: Aggregate of the statuses from all request lines.
Part Risk Score: Based on part-level health indicators.
Accordion Sections (Editable in Request):
Account, Contacts & Account Owner
Shipping Information
IDs
Tags
Note: These same accordions appear in each request line as well.
Editing a field at the Request level will sync that value to all associated Request Lines.
Request Lines Tab Overview
The Request Line captures detailed part-level requirements.
Every accordion from the Request tab is present, plus additional accordions/fields.
Accordions:
Account, Contacts, and Account Owner
Shows associated Account
Allows manual addition of a Contact
Option to assign additional users with roles like:
Buyer
BDR
Manager
Secondary Sales
All changes here are saved at the line level.
Opportunity & Requirement
Fields include:
Requirement Type, Requirement Level, Requirement Source
Notes
Mfr Part # (MPN), Manufacturer, Description, Internal Part #
Quantity Requested, Need By Date
Target Price, Min/Max Qty
Optional Part Information
Date Code (Year/Week)
Packaging
ECCN US / Europe, HTSUSA.
Point 15 in RFQ, Quotes.docx mentions the additional HTSUSA field and the change to ECCN Europe. 
Optional Account Preferences
Preferences like:
Black Labels
Partial Reels/Tubes
Missing Manufacturer Labels
Warranty Accepted by Sales
Part Condition Accepted by Sales
Shipping Information
Delivery Location
Full Address
Incoterms, Incoterm City
Ship Via
Option: "Ship all part numbers together?" toggle
Pricing
Currency, Target Price, Market Price, Regular Buying Price
Unit Type
Requested/Specified Alternatives
Add alternative MPNs with Manufacturer & Description
IDs
Add Customer RFQ ID 
Tags
Add relevant custom tags per line

Refer to the attached design for the exact field names and the UI layout. 
We will also revisit the mandatory fields based on Connect/xCRM mappings.

column6:
Summary
The Create a Request module enables users to generate Single/multiple RFQ items by adding request lines either manually or via file upload. The user journey consists of selecting a customer, entering request line details, and optionally triggering quote creation alongside the request.
Step 1: Account & Contact Selection
Select the Customer Account and associated Contact (mandatory fields)
Contact info (country, phone, email) is auto-populated
Step 2A: Add Lines Manually
Mandatory Fields:
Manufacturer Part Number (MPN)
Quantity
Optional Fields:
Targeted Cost per Unit, Targeted Total Cost, Need By Date
Packaging Type, Lead Time*, Date Code (Year/Week), QC Level
If MPN does not exist in SE, the user should be allowed to create a part/enter an MPN manually
Additional Quote Details (optional):
Cost per Unit, Total Cost, Estimated Freight, Miscellaneous Cost, Expiry Date
If cost per unit is entered, both Request and Quote will be created via backend APIs in the same transaction
Users can:
Add multiple request lines
Duplicate existing lines
Remove individual lines
Step 2B: Upload a File
Drag & drop or browse to upload a CSV or Excel file of request lines
Once uploaded:
System prompts user to validate file columns to project headers (e.g., MPN → Mfr Part #, QTY → Quantity)
Note - We need to reuse the auto-mapping logic, support the same format, and support the max size validation that exists currently for BoM.
User confirms mappings and continues
Step 3: Submit
Upon clicking Create, system triggers appropriate APIs to generate the Request (and optionally Quote)
Redirects user to the Request Details View 

Excel File - 
 
Design

column6:
As a System Administrator
I want to track sourcing request status changes automatically
So that workflow progress is accurately reflected

Acceptance Criteria:
Automatic status updates based on workflow events: 
"Open" → "Offers Requested" when first offer requested
"Offers Requested" → "Offers Received" when first offer recorded
"Offers Received" → "Recommendation Ready" when offer recommended (recommended - not shown on the chevrons on the hero)
"Recommendation Ready" → "Approval Requested" when submitted for approval
"Approval Requested" → "Approved" when approved
"Approval Requested" → "Rejected" when rejected
"Approved" → "PO Created" when purchase order generated
"PO Created" → "Closed" when PO confirmed/delivered
 Log all status changes in activity timeline
 Status history maintained for audit
 Cannot manually change status (workflow-driven only)
Show the status on hero card in the form of chevrons as shown on the design with all the aggregation at each step for various offers together from the offers table
As well show the most forward status highlighted above the chevrons. 

Status Flow:
Open → Offers Requested → Offers Received → 
Approval Requested → Approved → PO Created → Closed

Design guidance :

column4: Sourcing Request - Create a task to indicate a Sourcing request is assigned to a Buyer
column6:
Summary:
As a salesperson or manager, I want a Task to be created for the Buying Agent when a Purchase Request is created by checking the "create a task" option so that the sourcing activity is formally tracked and deadlines are not missed.

Acceptance Criteria:
When a user creates a Purchase Request and opts to create a task, the system creates a task to that buying agent and indicates that a purchase request has been created 
The new Task must be assigned to the Buying Agent selected in the PR modal.
The Task's content should include the Purchase Request ID and the Part Number.
The Task's due date should be set to the "Offer Needed by" date from the Purchase Request.

Design Guidance :

column4: Offers - Display open offers table on Purchase requests
column6:
Summary : 
As a purchaser
I want to view all offers in a comprehensive table format within the offers tab
So that I can compare supplier responses and manage the sourcing process

Acceptance criterion : 
Display "Offers" tab with comprehensive table layout showing all offers
 Show table columns: Offer ID | Date Created, Supplier | Location, Mfr Part # | Manufacturer, Lead Time | Date code, Requested Quantity vs Offered, Unit Price, Expires, Status
 Include offer summary footer: "X Offer(s) Requested • Y Received • Z Pending Response"
 Show "No offers responses recorded yet" message when table is empty
 Support column sorting by clicking any header
 Display three-dot action menu for each offer row
The three dot action menu should contain the following actions - Edit offer, recommend offer, unrecommend offer, request for purchasing, reject for purchase and delete offer 
 Support bulk selection with checkboxes to only support delete offer records for now 

Design guidance : 
https://www.figma.com/design/v5c0jzDP5riw5RFBNzIyqe/Chip-1-Designs?node-id=34820-80362&t=Ia0vGELGkbvH9F7l-0

column4: Offers - Approval through Request for Purchasing
column6:
Summary 
As a Purchasing Agent
I want to request an offer for purchase
So that it can be reviewed and approved before creation of a PO

Notes : 
REQUEST FOR PURCHASE = Formal approval workflow
ONLY this action triggers the approval workflow
Requires justification and routing to approver
Formal submission for PO creation authorisation
PO creation requires this formal approval

Acceptance Criteria:
 "Request for Purchase" action available for offers with status "Received" and "Recommended"
Request Modal (as per design):
Approver dropdown 
Date picker (e.g., "Nov 12, 2025")
Notes text area
"Cancel" and "Send" buttons
After Submission:
Status: "Requested for Purchase" (orange badge)
Green button appears: "Request for Purchasing Sent"
Lock offer (cannot edit)
Timeline entry: "Submitted for approval to [Approver]"
Notify approver (Covered in notifications flow)

Design guidance :

Summary 
As a Purchasing Manager
I want to approve a purchase request
So that purchasing can proceed with PO creation

Acceptance Criteria:
 Receive notification: "Offer from [Supplier] requested for purchase by [Agent]"
 Access to complete request details: 
 Offer details (supplier, pricing, quantity, lead time, terms)
 Purchase justification from agent on various checklists needed.
 Comparison to other offers received
 Part specifications
 Attached documents
 Linked sales requests and customer requirements
 "Approve" action available
 Automatically create Purchase Order upon approval
 System updates offer status to "Approved for purchase"
 Update Open Offers table: show "Approved for purchase" status
 Update Sourcing Request status to "Approved for purchase"
should result in creation of a purchase order automatically 

Reject Offer
As a Purchasing Manager
I want to reject purchase request
So that inappropriate purchases are prevented
Acceptance Criteria:
Rejection Action:
Click "Reject Offer for Purchase" from actions menu
Rejection modal opens
Required Fields:
Rejection reason (dropdown)
Detailed feedback (text area, required)
Recommendations (text area, optional)
"Cancel" and "Reject" buttons
After Rejection:
Status: "Rejected for Purchase" (red X icon)
Unlock offer for editing
Unlock other offers


Covered in notifications flow : (future)
 Send notification to purchasing agent: "Your purchase request for [Supplier] offer has been approved"

Design guidance :

Summary
As a developer, I would like to enhance the Notification Service capability in the chip1 app to accommodate several new capabilities, as explained in the following section.
Acceptance Criteria
After completion of the task, the following features should be implemented - 
The new CRM app should be capable of triggering notifications on various events
The list of events shall be defined later
Notification Center
Should support notification read/unread
Notification Banner - Browser Push notification
Real-time notification centre update through WebSocket
Notification preferences
Architecture Guidance
fn-notifications repo
https://github.com/altirllc/fn-notification-liquibase
https://github.com/altirllc/fn-notification-publisher
https://github.com/altirllc/fn-notification-dispatcher
https://github.com/altirllc/fn-notification-common-service
https://github.com/altirllc/fn-notification-api-hub
Architecture Doc - here
Notifications Kafka topic - doc
fn-notifications Overview - recording
API Hub - here (requires altir VPN)
Figma design - herech

Scope of Changes
User Onboarding
The fn-notifications is a multi-tenant service. It is managing its own tenants and users. The Chip1 tenant is currently configured, and several functionalities are linked to it. Now, we also need to onboard the new crm users in the Chip1 tenant.
Steps
1. The bulk user onboarding API can be used.
2. The notification user ID should be saved in the crm_users collection.
Notification Center
The notification center stores all the notifications generated by the app. It helps users to revisit the notifications and take necessary actions.
Figma - side pane, list view
 
 
Steps
1. The essential capability is already developed. Let's review the APIs
2. The API should support message read/unread
Notification Banner
This is the Browser push notification capability. The basic configuration is ready.
 
Steps
1. Let's verify the existing configuration.
Real-time update of Notification Center
When the notification is generated, it should appear in the notification list view in case the application is open. A websocket implementation should take care of it. This will prevent the screen from refreshing to fetch new notifications when the app is opened.
Enahancement
1. Need to add context information of the entity. It will help the app to fetch the entity details based on the context details, i.e., context_type, context_id.
 
Steps
1. Let's configure the websocket in the API GW, 
2. The backend shall push the newly created notification to the web app through the websocket.
Notification preferences
Users can select their preferred notification channels. The essential implementation is already in place.
Steps
1. Let's review the APIs and test.
Notification Trigger events
The chip1 services should trigger the notification events. More details shall be added later.   FYI.
 

API Guidance

column6:
Summary
Functional task Create an Account (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-1618)
As a developer, I want to develop a capability to fetch the company details.
Acceptance Criteria
After completion of the development
A set of endpoints shall be created to manage the companies for the below functionalities
CURD operations on Company
Integration with the external system (ZoomInfo APIs) to fetch the company details
Architecture Guidance
Data model here (Two collections company & globalCompany )
UI mock figma

There are two data sources of company records - 
External System - This is the primary source. The data must be fetched from the external system, like ZoomInfo, and then it will be saved in the application DBs.
Database - 
i. globalcompany collection in MongoDB, data fetched from the external system is saved here
ii. company collection in MongoDB, the user provided company details are saved here
iii. company index in the OpenSearch, contains the company data and support type-ahead company search
As per the functional flow - 
The company text field in the account creation screen should support the type-ahead search by company name.
The backend will search the result in the OpenSearch.
If a company is unavailable, a user shall create a company. The company record shall be saved in the OpenSearch company index and the MongoDB company collection. The partial flag is set to be false in both places
The company enrich data 
On user demand, the backend service will call the ZoomInfo Enrich API for the company details.
The data shall be stored in globalcompany, company  collections in MongoDB and company  index in OpenSearch. The partial flag is set to be true in the company collection and company index.

Need to explore the ZoomInfo APIs, here is the doc
The company collection will store the ultimateParentCompany along with the immediate parentCompany . It will be easy to form the company hierarchy structure.
When a new company record is fetched from the ZoomInfo system, the backend service will check the other companies in the hierarchy and update the ultimateParentCompany information if required.
An account can link with a company.

There is a change in approach regarding company search in account creation use case.
The external company search won't happen during account creation process.
The company field shall support type-ahead search to find companies. There will be an icon beside this to open a modal that creates a new company. The user can create a new company if it is unavailable. The new company record is saved in the company collection.
The company enrich data shall be pulled from the external source (ZoomInfo or other) on demand. There will be a such feature in the application, that will enable ICP to pull such data. The enrich data is saved in globalcompany collection. Unlike part search, it will not happen automatically.
Company search will not be part of the global search.
Om Sreenivasan to analyze how ZoomInfo is integrated with the CRM.
The CRM company data migration is not needed.

The following APIs are to be developed in chip1-transaction and chip1-account microservice



API
Purpose
Notes
[POST] /companies
Create a company
The record is saved in company collection
The record is saved in company index
[PATCH] /companies
Update a company
The record is updated in company collection and company index
[GET] /companies/details
A record is fetched from company collection
The record shall be fetched from company collections.
[GET] /companies/full-details
A record fetched form company and globalcompany collection.
The record shall be fetched from company and globalcompany collections.
The partial flag indicates enrich data is fetched or not.
If not, then the endpoint shall fetch the enrich data and save in globalcompany, company  collections in MongoDB and company  index in OpenSearch
/transaction/searches/global/COMPANY
Company search in the DB to support the type-ahead search
The search will be performed in the OpenSearch company index
Supported search attribute
company name
[POST] /companies/global
Company search in the ZoomInfo system to save the record
The search will be performed in the ZoomInfo system with company attributes.
The search result shall be saved in globalcompany, company  collections in MongoDB and company  index in OpenSearch
API Guidance

column6:
Summary
As a developer, I would like to develop a feature that allows mc1 users to save multiple sourcing plans in a project.
Acceptance Criteria
After the development is completed, the MC1 app should allow users to manage multiple sourcing plans in a project.
Architecture Guidance
UI design - figma
Data model -  
ProjectSourcingPlan collection - It stores the sourcing plan details.
SourcingPlan collection - It stores the sourcing plan items.
Sourcing Plan management
Each project must have a default sourcing plan, created by the system during project creation.
Users shall be allowed to create sourcing plans and manage them manually.
The following APIs should be introduced -

Purpose
Notes

Copying an existing sourcing plan
A new sourcing plan is created by copying another one

Adding project line items in the sourcing plan

An sourcing plan item is updated

Functional task - yet to define
As a developer, I will design and implement a new "Multi-RFQ Project" capability within the platform, complementing the existing "BOM" and "Excess" project types. This feature will enable users to seamlessly upload existing BOM templates, which the platform will then process to automatically generate RFQs for all parts listed in the file. A key differentiator from the existing auto-RFQ feature in BOM projects is that RFQs will be triggered for all parts by default, streamlining the procurement process.
Acceptance Criteria
After the completion of the development process, the platform should introduce a new Multi-RFQ project capability.
Architecture Guidance
Backend Changes 
Project Creation
A new project type should be added.  
Need clarification on the different options for the "Multi-RFQ" type on the UI.   
Tracking project health is required.
The upload template would remain the same.

Project Details 
There is no change needed in the project details for the "Multi-RFQ" project, as the existing details should hold good.
The project parts should be analysed and trigger the automatic RFQ. Unlike the BOM project, the auto-rfq should be triggered for all parts in the project.

Suggestions from Andrey:
Use mongo collection to store above configuration along with linked permissions. Each mongo document is config for one entity type. Example:
//list of tabs
  "tabs": [
    {
      "names": ["summary"],
      "viewPermission": "VIEW_ACCOUNT_SUMMARY_TAB",
      "editPermission": "EDIT_ACCOUNT_SUMMARY_TAB"
    },
    {
      "names": ["details"],
      "viewPermission": "VIEW_ACCOUNT_DETAILS_TAB",
      "editPermission": "EDIT_ACCOUNT_DETAILS_TAB",
      //group is set of sections with the same permission
      "groups": [
        {
          "sections": [
            {
              "name": "general",
              "paths": [ //field or field path with dots
                "name",
                "code",
                "otherNames",
                "regionalName",
                "type",
                "industries",
                "stage",
                "phones",
                "addresses",
                "emails"
                ...
              ]
            },
            {
              "name": "ids",
              "paths": [
                "externalIds"
              ]
            },
            {
              "name": "invoicing",
              ...
            }
            ...
          ],
          "viewPermission": "VIEW_ACCOUNT_DETAILS_GENERAL_SECTION",
          "editPermission": "EDIT_ACCOUNT_DETAILS_GENERAL_SECTION"
        },
        {
          "sections": [
            {
              "name": "commissions",
              "paths": [
                "commissions"
              ]
            }
          ],
          "viewPermission": "VIEW_ACCOUNT_DETAILS_COMMISSION_SECTION",
          "editPermission": "EDIT_ACCOUNT_DETAILS_COMMISSION_SECTION"
        }
      ],
    },
    {
      "names": ["hierarchy", "profile", "projects", "parts"...],
      "viewPermission": "VIEW_ACCOUNT_ENTITIES_TAB",
      "editPermission": "EDIT_ACCOUNT_ENTITIES_TAB"
    },
    {
      "names": ["listView"], //this "tab" is applied on list view to restrict some columns
       "listView": true,
      "groups": [
        {"sections": [{name: "anyName1", "paths": ["id", "name"..]}],"view/editPermission:".."}
        {"sections": [{name: "commissions","paths": ["commissions"]}],"view/editPermission:".."}
      ],
      "viewPermission": "VIEW_ACCOUNT_LIST",
      "editPermission": "EDIT_ACCOUNT_LIST"
    }
  ],
  //FAB actions
  "actions": [
    {
      "names": ["updateStatus", "reject"],
      "permission": "ACCOUNT_UPDATE_STATUS"
    },
    {
      "names": ["delete"],
      "permission": "DELETE_ACCOUNT"
    },
    {
      "names": ["create"],
       "listView": true, //this action is applied to whole list view, not an account
      "permission": "EDIT_ACCOUNT"
    }
  ],
  "viewPermission": "VIEW_ACCOUNT",
  "editPermission": "EDIT_ACCOUNT"
}
We can define permissions on different levels (account-level, tab level, group level or section level). a lower level (e.g. section) takes precedence over a higher one (account level permission).

Notes
Role-wise UI tabs/sections/actions accessibility is defined here.
Sai suggested defining default permissions and letting the frontend derive and render UI accordingly. However, I'm concerned that calculating permissions in client-side JavaScript could expose them to users via the browser. We can discuss with UI developers.

API changes
The API response should include the UI sectional permission. The frontend should honor it and render the UI accordingly. Here is the additional permission data snippet that must be included in the existing API responses

Currently, endpoints are checking the existing permissions(delete RFQ, view RFQ, delete quote, etc.) to allow executions. It may shift to the above idea to determine the operation eligibility.

Example of responses as per above Mongo collection suggestion:
For account details view: (from Andrey)
{

  "data": { ...account details object...},
  "permissions": {
    "tabs": [
      {
        "name": "summary",
        "scope": "VIEW"
      },
      {
        "name": "details",
        "sections": [
          {"name":  "general", "scope":  "EDIT", "paths":  ["name","code", "otherNames"...]},
          {"name":  "ids", "scope":  "EDIT", "paths":  ["externalIds"]},
          ...
          {"name":  "commissions", "scope":  "VIEW", "paths":  ["commissions"]}
        ],
        "scope": "VIEW"
      },
      {
        "name": "hierarchy",
        "scope": "VIEW"
      },
      {
        "name": "profile",
        "scope": "VIEW"
      }
    ],
    "actions": [
      {
        "name": "updateStatus",
        "scope": "VIEW"
      },
      {
        "name": "reject",
        "scope": "VIEW"
      }
    ]
  }
}
For account list view: (From Andrey)
{

  "data": [
    {"id": "1"..., "permissions": {"actions": [ //only actions are applicable for list item
      {
        "name": "updateStatus",
        "scope": "EDIT"
      },
      {
        "name": "reject",
        "scope": "EDIT"
      }
    ]}},
    ...
  ],
  "permissions": {
    "exceptPaths": ["commissions"], //it will be calculated from listView=true tabs - it's a list of paths, that should be excluded based on user permissions
    "actions": [ //only listView=true actions are applied here
      {
        "name": "create",
        "scope": "VIEW"
      }
    ]
  }
}

Testing guidance
This feature is common approach for all entities. There is not final decision yet, how the sections should be accessed for each role - it will be defined later. For the testing purposes only ACCOUNT config will be defined. Just to cover the most use cases, each existing role (super admin, sales, global view) will have different set of permissions:
super admin (`crm-super-admin@yopmail.com` in DEV env) - can VIEW and EDIT all tabs and sections in Account. New account can be created.
sales role (`crm-sales-user@yopmail.com` in DEV) - view only all tabs, except Details tab: here General sections is not visible, but Commissions section is editable, rest of sections are view only. No FAB for create account 
global-view role (crm-global-view@yopmail.com) - view only all tabs and section, except section Details → Commissions - it is not visible. No FAB for create account 
Note: UI section permissions are also used by backend: if some section is not visible on UI, backend response also shouldn't have corresponding fields; if some fields are not editable, save/patch will be failed if such field will be edited.

Here are the identified points for performance improvement in the Connect Service
1. Separate inbound and outbound messages and add listeners accordingly
2. Add a separate migration topic for bulk publishing and add listeners accordingly
3. Add logs to calculate time taken for each operation (Jolt, reconciliation, etc)
4. Caching:
    4.1 Reduce the db calls (In-memory/ Remote) for entity-id-mapping, Id reconciliation, etc
    4.2 Api key caching, to avoid round trips to identity service to validate incoming api key
5. Increase processing rate per partition (consumer should have more resource utilization)
6. Create an alert and send a notification when consumer lag increases by a certain threshold

column4: Authorization - Handling Roles, Permissions, and Datagroups
column6:
Summary
As a developer, I would like to enhance the identity service to manage user roles and permissions in the new CRM system.
Acceptance Criteria
Architecture Guidance
Data model
Refer to [Spike] CRM App - Enhance Identity service (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-1416) for Data model details.
CRM accounts - data model
It's a MongoDB model and holds CRM account data.
CRM users, roles, permission, data group - data model
It's a MySql model and holds CRM users and other records.
A new schema crm shall be created
Create tables through liquibase
roles, permission, and rolePermission records can be created through liquibase
Datamodel summary


Name
Description
User
Stores CRM user records
role
CRM roles.
permission
CRM permissions. Granular level actions
rolePermission
Mapping between roles & permissions
userRole
A user associated with roles, one to many mapping.
type → DEFAULT , a role is assinged during user creation.
type → DATAGROUP , an additional role is assinged for data groups
dataGroup
A datagroup is formed with users, roles, and entities. Here is the idea -
A user shall have ONLY one role in a data group
A user shall have access to all entities belong to a datagroup
Entity types are ACCOUNT, LEAD, etc.
type → ACCOUNT denotes all entitis(rfq, quote, SO, invoice) of this account.
individual entities (rfq, quote, SO, invoice) as well.

dataGroupEntity
Mapping between datagroup and entities
dataGroupUser
Mapping between datagroup and users
API development - All APIs shall be created in the chip1-account microservice
Records management APIs
Develop APIs to manage roles, permissions, datagroup records
Authorization APIs
crmUserLogin API API - the response returns a bunch of information for the JWT token
{
    "data": {
        "dataGroups": {
            "data-group-2": "SALES_MANAGER" //datagroup and the respective role of that user in that datagroup
},
        "globalRoles": [
            {
                "region": "ALL",
                "role": "ADMIN"
            },
            {
                "region": "US-region-id",
                "role": "SALES_MANAGER"
            }
        ]
    }
}
Consumer - The Identity service
The purpose is to populate JWT claims. The dataGroup shall filter entities (accounts, RFQ, quote, SO, Invoice) in the downstream services.
Authorization API is deprecated and the authorization API's data is included in token claim itself.  authorization API - the response returns a list of permissions for each account to a user.
{
  "authorization": {
    "data group 1": [ "project write", "po write" ] // list of permissions
    }
  }
}
Consumer - All downstream services
All microservices enforce authorization by referring to a list of permissions at the account level.
Notes - 
All APIs shall call this endpoint to enforce the authorizations hence it should be very performant. Redis caching can be considered here to reduce the response time.
Save all roles and permissions in Redis
Save user roles in Redis

Proposed Datagroup, Roles, and Permissions
Data Groups
We will create one data group at region level initially
For regions who are ok to share across their region that can stay as is. if they want to share at sub region level (country) we can create more data groups and migrate based on country code of account
For regions who want super restricted like Europe, we can create data group per each sales person if they dont budge
 
Below are the roles and permissions we came up with, we may add/remove permissions once we get more details

One Account with multiple regions in CRM should be split to that many accounts in new system, need to handle it connect appropriately and move to respective data groups

New Proposal
1. Roles
  1. Super Admin - No data groups are applied, everything is allowed - C level executives
  2. Limited View - Limited view of any entity, not all sections are allowed
  3. Full View - Full view of any entity, all sections are allowed
  4. Editor - Can edit any given entity for which permission is allowed

2. Access
  1. Damon, Sasan (COO, CEO) - global-role [{region:all, role:superAdmin}]
  2. Pascal (Sales Head) - global-role:[{region:all, role:limited-view}, {region:DE, role:full-view]
  3. Jacque (sales head, US) - global-role:[{region:us, role:full-view}]
  4. Milo (sales, US) - global-role:[{region:us, role:limited-view}]
  5. Jose (admin, Mexico) - global-role:[{}]

3. Data group
  1. dg1:{accounts:[1,2,3,4], user:[Milo, editor, {Jose, editor}]} - 1,2,3,4
  2. dg2:{accounts:[A,B,C,D,2], user:[{Pascal, editor}, {Aki, editor}]} - A, B, C, D are Germany accounts

 
1. Damon - Get Accounts - As global role is super admin, everything else is ignored
2. Pascal - Get Accounts for DE - As global role is full view, data groups not applied, show all accounts for DE region
3. Jacque - Get Accounts for US - As global role is limited view, data groups not applied, show all accounts for US region
4. Milo
  1. Get accounts for DE - No global role for region, dg1 data group applied, no matching region so nothing visible
  2. Get accounts for US -  global role for region, can see all accounts in the US region
  3. Edit Account 1 - Global role is limited-view does not have edit permission, look for data group permission. dg1 has editor permission and 1 is in dg1, so edit is allowed
  4. Edit Account 5 - Global role is limited-view does not have edit permission, look for data group permission. dg1 has editor permission and 5 is not in dg1, so edit is not allowed
5. Jose
  1. Get accounts for US - global role for region not there, data group dg1 has editor access, can see 1,2,3,4

Functional task Create an Account (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-1618)
As a developer, I would like to develop the capability of managing the accounts in the new CRM platform.
Acceptance criteria
After completion of the development
A set of endpoints shall be created to manage the contacts for the below functionalities
CURD operations on accounts
Account list, filter, and quick search
Account sync to the existing CRM
Account summary (HERO section)
Entities (project, contact, parts, quotes, SO) filter by account
Architecture guidance
Data model here
UI mock figma
The existing APIs are defined here. The following improvements are required in chip1-account microservice



API
Purpose
Notes
[POST] /accounts
An account is created
An API already exists and need to evaluate if any changes are needed
The record should be indexed in the OpenSearch to support quick search
The connect service shall sync the new account record to the existing CRM.    FYI.

[PATCH] /accounts
An account is updated
An API already exists and need to evaluate if any changes are needed
The update should be propagated to the OS
The connect service shall sync the updated contact record to the existing CRM.    FYI.

[GET/POST] /accounts
A list of accounts
Two endpoints are present for the same purpose
[POST] /internal/accounts 
[GET] /internal/accounts 
Let's create a single endpoint for all kind of filters
b. Remove /internal in the endpoint and make it open for all
A list of accounts should be displayed
The list should support filtering by Account Type, Country, Region, Status along with Prospect , Active , Passive Closed , Blacklist users.

[GET] /accounts/{id}
Get an account by id
There is an existing endpoint /internal/accounts/{id} , let's reuse in this purpose.
The existing response shall be enhanced to contain the HERO section data.

[GET] /accounts/email/{email}
To validate an existing email in the system
The UI validates an existing email while a new account is created.
[GET] /transaction/searches/global/
Account quick search
There is an existing /transaction/searches/global/ API for global search.
The existing global search API should be enhanced to support account quick search
The quick search attributes are yet to design (  to confirm)
Entity filter
project
parts
quotes
Orders
Contacts
In the account details screen, the given list of entities should be filtered by accounts
The entity list API should be handling it with datagroup filter
Connect Service changes (  FYI)
The account creation & update in the new platform must be synced to the CRM.
A new account shall be created along with a new/existing contact.
The downstream service should sync the account first and then contact with the account_id in it (a similar approach lead_account_and_contact can be taken here).
The existing flow of account creation & update in the existing CRM will remain the same.
The lead_account flow - There are two ways to create the lead_account entity
A new user sign-up happens in the mc1 app hence, a new mc1Account and a new mc1User are created in the mc1
The new account shall be stored as a Prospect account in the account collection
The mc1 triggers the lead_account_and_contact event to sync the lead_account and lead_contact in the existing CRM.
A lead_account can be created in the new CRM app (yet to define  )
The new account shall be created in the new CRM app with account stage Prospect 
The Prospect account shall be synced with the existing CRM as lead_account

Other considerations - 
The existing account in the mc1 app should be indexed in the OpenSearch
Two endpoints [POST] /internal/accounts & [GET] /internal/accounts should be refactored to support filtered list view of accounts.
The downstream service introduces a new attribute source in the account entity. The source attribute indicates the system (new CRM / existing CRM) of the record creation. The data sync between the systems can be determined based on this attribute.
The account record should be able to save the parentAccount and ultimateParentAccount data. On saving, pick the parent and find its parent recursively and save it.

CRM & new systems should stop the event trigger if the operation does not originate there.
The lead_account flow should not be handled in the new CRM now.   
The prospect account is not lead_account. The prospect account should be considered as a regular account in the new and existing CRM

Summary
Functional task not yet defined
As a developer, I want to develop the capability to build the company hierarchy and account hierarchy details.
Acceptance Criteria
After completion of the development
Two endpoints shall be developed to manage the Company hierarchy and Account hierarchy
Architecture Guidance
Data model here (Two collections company & account )
UI mock - Company Hierarchy Accounts Hierarchy

The following APIs should be developed in the chip1-accounts service



API
Purpose
Notes
[GET] /companies/hierarchy
UI displays the company hierarchy. 
The hierarchy details shall be fetched from the company collection
[GET] /accounts/hierarchy
UI displays the account hierarchy. 
The hierarchy details shall be fetched from the account collection
[PATCH] /accounts/hierarchy
Adding a parent or children  to an account
{  
"accountId":"acc_0"
  "parent": "acc_1",  
  "children": ["acc_2", "acc_3" ]
}
[DELETE]
/accounts/hierarchy
Delete children or parent
{  
"accountId":"acc_0"
  "parent": true,  
  "children": ["acc_2", "acc_3" ]
}

A node must have only one parent
The API shouldn't allow adding siblings or ultimate parents.
Computation -
The company and account entities have parentId and ultimateParentId. Refer to the hierarchy below
 
The company hierarchy shows ONLY companies and does not show accounts in it.
The account hierarchy shows ONLY accounts and does not show companies in it.
To display the entity hierarchy (company, account), the backed service should fetch all entities having the same ultimateParentId.
The list of entities has a parentId through which the entity level can be determined.
The list of entities shall be passed to the UI to compute the hierarchy.

column6:
Objective
Standardize validation rules and error messages across all form fields in the new CRM, including user, contact, account, project, and profile creation/edit screens.

Acceptance Criteria

Validation Rules
Required Text Inputs
 Format: <Field Name> is required.
 Example: First Name is required.
Dropdown/Select Fields
 Format: Select a <Field Name>.
 Example: Select a Project Type.
Dropdown/Select Fields (Multi-Select)
 Format: Select at least one <Field Name>
 Example: Select at least one Language
Note: If the field name is plural (e.g., Languages), use the singular form in the error message (e.g., Language).
Invalid Format
 Format: Enter a valid <Field Name>.
 Example: Enter a valid Email Address.
Max Length for all text boxes (Limit all applicable text fields to 100 characters)
 Format: <Field Name> cannot exceed 100 characters.
 Example: Company Name cannot exceed 100 characters.
Max Length for Notes
Example: Notes cannot exceed 500 characters.
First Name
Required
Max Length: 100
Last Name
Required
Max Length: 100
Email Address
Required
Must be valid email format
Example: user@example.com
Phone Number
Required
Must be a valid phone number
Supports international format (reuse MC1 validation)
EXT
Optional
Max Length: 6
LinkedIn URL
Optional
Must contain “linkedin.com”
Format: Enter a valid LinkedIn profile URL
Company
Max Length: 100
Account
Type-ahead search
Error: Select a valid Account
Contact
Type-ahead search
Error: Select a valid Contact
Project Name
Max Length: 100
Account Name
Max Length: 100
Title
Max Length: 100
Suffix
From predefined dropdown (e.g., Jr., Sr., III)
Department, Office, Manager, Role, Permission
Dropdown
Error: Select a Department / Office / Manager / Role / Permission
Timezone
Dropdown
If required: Select a Timezone
Project Type, Project Stage
Dropdown
Error: Select a Project Type / Project Stage
Need By Date / Date of Birth
Must be valid date
Error: Enter a valid date
Languages
Multi-select
If required: Select at least one Language
Nationality
Optional
Dropdown
Error: Select a Nationality
Position (IP address field)
Use example formatting only (e.g., 123.45.67.89)
Street, City, State, Zip (if added)
Max Length: 100 (if text)

Objective:
 Update the Account List View to align with the latest CRM data and improve usability by simplifying columns and enhancing key actions.
Changes:
Remove Column:
Remove the Tasks column from the list view.
Add Columns:
Add Engagement and Potential columns (based on CRM scoring logic).
Retain existing # of Projects and # of Orders columns.
From these four columns (Engagement, Potential, Projects, Orders), move 2 into the column overflow menu to avoid visual clutter. Prioritize display based on CRM usage feedback.
Column Replacement:
The first column (ID) does not have backend support.
Replace with Account Type in the format as shown in below design
Update Location Field:
Display City, State, Country in a single column instead of using a separate “Location” column.
Country remains available as a filter.
Email Interaction:
Add an email icon next to the Primary Contact.
Clicking the icon should open the user’s default mail client with the contact’s email pre-filled.
Account Stages:
Prospect, Active, Closed, Blacklisted are static account stages.
Active and Passive are dynamically derived:
Active: Last active date within 180 days.
Passive: Last active date older than 180 days.
7.  Column Name Revisions: 
Replace Stage with Status.
Replace Last Activity with Last Contacted By.
8. Add Account Owner/BDM
Introduce a new column Account Owner/BDM - We can put this in overflows if no data is present


Reference Design - We will retain the existing columns on # of Projects and # of Orders

Summary : 
Users can navigate into Accounts from the left menu, click into any company (like Siemens Healthineers), and land on the Profile tab. They’ll see an About section which has general info about the parent company but not the branch or the franchise, auto-filled using a LLM tool like ChatGPT or Gemini or Claude as well as financials taken from the general public information or using an LLM. 

Acceptance criterion : 
Navigation :
The left sidebar has an Accounts icon 
Clicking it opens a list of all Accounts.
Clicking a account name (Siemens Healthineers) takes the user to that company’s detail page.
The Profile tab opens by default.
The profile has About section that talks about the company and its financials  

About section content : 
Shows key company info:
Company Name
Address and Location
Federal EIN
Parent Company name
Number of Employees
Website and LinkedIn links
Description of the company 

Description of the company : 
Displays a summary generated using ChatGPT or similar.
Summarises what the company does, their focus, and value in simple language.
Helps users understand the company at a glance.
Includes clickable website and LinkedIn links.
If no data available just put up "No data available" note

Financials Section
Appears within the About section.

Annual Revenue in a graph against last 7 years

column4: Project Enhancement - Process same IPN in the bom file
column6:
Summary
As a developer, I would like to enhance the existing project capability to handle the same IPNs in the BOM excel file.
Acceptance Criteria
After completing the task, parts in the BOM file with identical IPNs will be treated as alternative parts, excluding the first occurrence.
Architecture Guidance
Current behaviour
Currently, in the mc1 app, there is no way to upload a BOM or add a part as an alternative item in the project. All the parts in the BOM file are considered project items.

Expected behaviour
As an enhancement, the mc1 app shall allow uploading parts as an alternative through the BOM file upload.
Updated Data model here

The Internal Part Number (IPN) in the BOM file serves as a key identifier for determining alternative parts. When multiple parts share the same IPN:
First occurrence: The first part with a unique IPN will be designated as the primary project line item.
Subsequent occurrences: All subsequent parts with the same IPN will be automatically considered alternative parts for the primary item.
The alternative MPNs should be resolved for the part details through async process
The alternative MPN details must be saved in the item.alternatives inline of the project collection.
Idea of managing lineitem and alternatives - 
On the UI, the user should have the capability to add alternatives to a lineitem.
The user should be able to replace an alternative part for the lineitem. In the project collection, the project.item  and item.alternative[]  should swap the places. 
API Guidance

column4: New CRM ID generation and xCRM ID sync for Account, and Contact
column6:
Summary
Functional task - NA
As a developer, I would like to generate a user-friendly ID for account and contact entities. 
Acceptance Criteria
After completing the development, the new CRM should be able to generate a user-friendly ID for accounts and contacts.
Architecture Guidance
ID generation strategy 
MySQL AUTO_INCREMENT driven. Proposed table structure: id, entity_type, entity_id, updated_on
Accounts, Contacts entities should be covered now.
(Out of scope) Other entities (rfq, quotes, SO, Invoice, etc) shall be handled later. The entity_id reset capability must be present. New CRM ID generation for transactional entities (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-2905)
Downstream Service
Develop the user-friendly ID generation capability for Account and Contact entities in the chip1-account service.
Let's introduce crmId attribute in the Accounts entity. The Contact entity already has accounts[].crmContactId.
User-friendly ID generation strategy
An entity is created in the new CRM: 
The user-friendly ID shall be generated and saved in the corresponding attributes.
Sync data to xCRM and generate an xCRM ID.
The xCRM ID shall be replaced user-friendly ID.
An entity is migrated from the xCRM - 
As the data migration carries the xCRM ID, no need to generate a user-friendly ID.
Enhancement(Out of Scope) - A similar approach shall be taken for other entities as well. The user-friendly <entity>_Number should be generated for new CRM generated entities.
Connect Service
New CRM generated entities must be synced to the xCRM.
The xCRM ID must be written back to the new CRM.

The new CRM IDs must be generated in such a way that they shouldn't be duplicates of the xCRM entity IDs.
The new CRM ID must be 7 digits.

Summary
As a developer, I would like to develop a feature in the new CRM that would enable a user to manage an Offer. A purchase team member creates an Offer to initiate the procurement process. The system must support two ways to add an offer:
Request an Offer: It allows formally request a quote from one or more suppliers. This creates an offer in a "Requested" state.
Record an Offer: It allows users to manually log offer details received externally (phone, email). This creates an offer in a "Recorded" state.
Each Offer represents a single part with single pricing. If the supplier quotes multiple prices for a part, then multiple offers should be recorded.
Use case example - 
An RFQ is requested for a part.
A purchase request must be created for the same. It will inform the purchase team about the part procurement.
The purchasing agent will initiate an Order Request, which involves soliciting quotes from one or more suppliers. An offer shall be created with the requested status.
The supplier responds with 2 pricing options for the same part. 
The existing Offer (with requested status) shall be updated with one price, and the new status would be recorded 
A new offer shall be created with another price with recorded status
Both offers should be linked to the Purchase Request.

Here is the handshaking between Sales module & Purchase module.
A RFQ is created by a customer, and it is assigned to a sales person.
The sales person is reviewing the RFQ and looking for the part. The sales person creates a Sourcing Request and intimate to the Purchase team.
A Purchasing team member starts working with the Sourcing Request and create Offers for multiple vendors.
Vendors come up with the item pricing to the member and it is updated in the offers.
Then the sales person reviews the available Offers and creates quote. The quote is shared with the customer.
The customer reviews the different options in a quote and agreed upon a price.
Once the sales person is received the confirmation, A Sales Order is created from a quote line.
Then the purchasing team member creates a Purchase Order refering to the Sales Order.
Then the item is shipped by the vendor to the chip1 warehouse. Once the item is received, A Receiving Line is created to the Purchase Order.
Then the Item shall go through the QC process.
Once the QC is done, the allocation shall happen. In this step, the receiving line quantity shall be assigned to the Sales Order Line.

column4: All Accounts – Search and Ownership Request
column6:
Design:  

Allow any CRM user to search across all accounts in their region and request ownership of one or more accounts. This enables a self-serve claim workflow without requiring admin intervention.
 
Search
Account Name is the only mandatory search field. All other fields are optional. Andrey handles the BE logic to return matching results. The UI reuses the existing All Accounts layout (tabs, columns, and row pattern) with no design changes.
Filters and Views apply only to the returned search results, not the full account list. If this scoping is not feasible, Filters and Views are removed entirely from this view.
 
Ownership Request Flow
The user selects one or more accounts from the search results and clicks "Request Ownership." A modal appears with the following fields:
Assign To (Manager): Required. User manually selects their manager from a dropdown. No automatic identification at this stage.
Description: Optional free-text field. Whatever the user enters here is carried into the task description.
On submission, a single task is created for the entire batch selection. Any mandatory task fields not captured in the modal (e.g., Status) are defaulted automatically (Status defaults to Open). The selected account names are included in the task context.
The assigned manager receives the task, reviews it, and manually navigates to each account to reassign ownership. There is no automated account reassignment at this stage.

The requesting user would get notified when ownership is granted.

Overview
Existing roles, permissions, DG - Authorization - Handling Roles, Permissions, and Datagroups (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-1460)
UI permission implementation - Authorization: UI Section permission (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-3211)
The existing roles, permissions, and DG are considered to be the base permissions.
The view is another level of data filtration on top of the base permissions.
The view filter shouldn't be applied to the global search
 
View My Data - My base permission
View by Region - 
Determine the list of regions through DG
Apply the further filter based on the user selection
View a Group - Determine the DGs based on base permissions
Impersonate - 
Design a data model for eligible users & their team to use this feature
The user collection contains the manager's information. It can be useful here
View My Data vs View by Region(all regions)
Architectural Guidance
figma - link
Global View control
The user's choice of view must be saved in the crm_users collection.
The following is the data model snippet. Only one view can be selected at a time.
Let's consider if the default view is required in the crm_user collection through data migration.
"view": {
    "default": true,
    "regions": ["DE", "US"],
    "team":["team001", "team002"]
    "impersonate": {
        "region": "US",
        "user": {
            "id": ""
        }
    }
}
The [PATCH] /users must be updated to save the view.
View My Data
This is the view based on the user's default permissions. No added data filtration is needed.
 
View by Region
A user is associated with regions that can be derived from the user_roles table. It has region_id.
sd in the chip1-accounts service to cater to the list of regions associated with a user.
Since the region details are empty in the user_roles table, all regions should be displayed in the modal. 
Data filtering shall happen based on the following
The base permission (existing roles, permissions, and DG)
The user selected regions (All entity collections must have region details)
 
View a Group
A team is a new entity introduced in the CRM. The team data model is defined here.
A team has 3 designations: manager, teamlead, salesperson
Each designation corresponds to a specific level, which determines the extent of data visibility.
A manager can see the teamlead, and salesperson data.
A teamlead can see salesperson data.
A salesperson can't see anyone else's data.
An endpoint should be developed in the chip1-accounts service to cater to the list of teams. Team Management (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-3582)
Data filtering shall happen based on the following
Data visibility varies based on the team member's designation level.
The user can't see their own data in this view.
 
Impersonate View 
Impersonation capability in CRM (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-2094)
Need to develop a user impersonation feature in the CRM app. This capability allows authorized users (e.g., admins/leadership) to simulate another user's experience, accessing the app's features and data as if they were the impersonated user. This feature facilitates effective oversight and management of business data on behalf of other users.

Add a flag to the crm_users collection to indicate users with impersonation capability, controlling the visibility of this option (Out of scope now. It will be handled on the UI)
The backend should fetch a list of relevant regions from the crm_users collection based on manager's details. (Out of scope. A user will see all available regions now)
A list of impersonated users shall be derived from the crm_users collection based on the manager details and user selected region. Let's add a region filter to the crm_user entity. [Need API change]
The Impersonation Capability feature is already developed.

API Hub PR
Follow this thread regarding the link1, link2

Summary

The new platform for chip1 has scenarios that need various data aggregations to be presented in dashboard and/or as entity summary. These can be querying various aspects of the system based on different personas and or entities in the system. 

Implement an API that allows data in dashboards to be pulled from various entities. Dashboard can have various aspects/cards, allow composition of responses based on the cards requested. 

Acceptance Criteria

Following would be the outcome for this
An API that will respond with data set needed for each dashboard card. 
An API that will respond with data set needed for each entity summary card.
The inputs for the API can include list of cards needed, the user for which they are needed and any entity applicable (if pulling for a give account)

Architecture Guidance
We can potentially have a single API that acts as a composite API that invokes different services.
The only reason we might want to separate the APIs at entity is considering that some entities are in different micro-services. This could also be solved by having a dashboard service associated with each entity/micro-service and then an overall composite service.
The cards can be configured on backend with any necessary fixed aspects of it. 
We could also have cards organized as card-templates that can help in determining which template to use for what role/entity. 
For all metrics cards keep duration as an input, so it gives us flexibility to call the same card with different input. E.g., for life time vs. last week or last month

Design Guidance:

Summary
Given the recent imposition of Tarffis by US and other countries, it has become a key element for all supply chain planning.  Implement a feature where one can see the impact of a tariffs when seeing a part in the context of project and eventually in a part as well.
Acceptance Criteria
Calculate the tariff applicable for a part based on the country of origin and the assembly location mentioned in the project. As part of this implementation 

Look for HTSUSA code on the part
Capture Assembly location (country) at a Project level
See if the HTSUSA code is in the exception list
If not in exception, compute what tariff is applicable based on the country of origin and assembly location. The assembly location becomes importing country.
Architecture Guidance
Preferably store the tariff mappings in a table for each country against the importing from country along with applicable tariffs. 
Might be good to store the tariff at a line level in the project
For a part when we have to show this, it can be based on an input of "importing country" and this can be be provided by UI based on either input or user profile.

At the top of the task list view, users see 8 fixed micro tiles. These tiles give a quick summary of high-volume task categories. They're not customisable yet. In the future, user should be able to personalise using data selection for these tiles.

Each tile shows:
Title of the workflow

Tiles are not configurable in this version. 

Logic:
 Each tile count = total open tasks (status ≠ "Completed") where task type or action matches a fixed list mapped to that tile. For example:
"Quote Follow Ups" = tasks with action = "Quote Follow Up"
"Account Outreach" = tasks with action = "Outreach"

column4: Tasks - Dropdown filter bar for the tasks list view
column6:
Summary:
The filter bar allows users to narrow down the visible task list by selecting one or more filter options. It supports multiple filter types like assignee, task status, priority, and action. Users can combine filters and reset them all at once using “Clear All”.
 Acceptance Criteria:
Filter bar displays a row of dropdown filters
Each dropdown shows a label and current selection
Filters include:
Assignee
Action
Priority
Status
Selecting any value from a dropdown immediately updates the list
Users can apply multiple filters simultaneously
"Clear All" resets all filters to their default state

Logic : The task list is filtered using these task fields:
Assigned to
Actions from the list of actions   
Priority (Low, Medium, High, Urgent)
Status (Open, In Progress, On Hold, Completed)

Design Guidance :

Summary:
Users can change the visual grouping of tasks in the list based on shared properties like action, assignee, or dates. This wont filter any data but rather a view control 
Acceptance Criteria:
Grouping options:
No Grouping
Action
Task Type
Assigned To
Status
Priority
Start Date
Due Date
Changing the group updates task list layout without filtering any data

Logic: Grouping uses the selected attribute (action, assignedTo, startDate) to organise the visible list into clusters. No filtering is applied — only sorting by the grouping field.

Design Guidance :

Summary:
 The task table is the primary way to scan and manage tasks. Each row represents a task and shows enough data to identify what needs to be done, who owns it, and when it’s due.
Acceptance Criteria:
Columns: (Can be customisable in the future, but for now fixed columns as mentioned below)
Task Title
Created By / Created Date
Assigned To / Assigned Date
Due Date / Start Date
Related Entity (tag + clickable ID)
Priority / Action (stacked)
Status
Last Updated
Users can select tasks, open detail view, or access quick actions per each row

Logic:
Assigned date = created date if task was assigned on creation; otherwise, it's when the first assignedto value was added
Start date is user-defined and separate from assignment logic
relatedEntity is derived from context.entityType + context.entityId

Design Guidance :

Summary:
When creating a task, users can choose to set a reminder to get notified before it's due. 
Acceptance criterion 
"Set Reminder" option appears in the task creation modal, located right below the Due Date.
Users can toggle it on or off.
Reminder is only shown if the priority is set to High or Urgent — it’s hidden for lower priorities.

Logic Once enabled:
Reminder will go out 1 day before the due date (if due date is 24+ hours away).
If due date is today, it’ll go out 1 hour before.

Communication channels : for now only email. 
The reminder will be sent via email, as in-app notifications aren't supported yet.
We will provide a template 
Reminder gets triggered automatically based on the selected due date and configuration.

column4: Tasks - FAB (floating action button options) (In progress)
column6:
Summary:
On every page of the Tasks, there’s a small floating + button in the bottom-right corner. It opens up shortcuts to do quick actions without leaving the screen. Options change according to the screen that is open
Acceptance Criteria (All Pages)
FAB is fixed to the bottom-right, responsive across all screens
Menu opens on click and overlays above table
Each FAB action behaves consistently:
Opens modal
Applies change to all selected tasks
Clears selection post action
Shows confirmation toast
“Create Task” always available when nothing is selected

FAB Actions General Behaviour (Applies to All Pages)
Always visible in bottom-right corner of the screen
Appears as a circular "+" button by default
Clicking opens an action menu
Actions available change based on selection state


State
Available FAB Options
No tasks selected
Create Task
One or more tasks selected
Add To, Add/Edit Start Date, Edit Due Date, Change Priority, Update Status, Archive Task
FAB on: Tasks List View
Actions Available (When Tasks Are Selected)
Add To (My Day, My Reminders, My Week)
Opens modal with radio options:
My Day
My Reminders
My Week
Optional comment box
Applies selected label to each task under user-specific date based data
Add/Edit Start Date
Opens date picker modal
Applies the selected Startdate to all selected tasks
Task rows update accordingly
Edit Due Date
Opens modal to pick a new due date
All selected tasks get their due date updated uniformly
Change Priority
Shows dropdown with: Low, Medium, High, Urgent
Bulk updates priority field for selected task.
Update Status
Dropdown with refined status values: Open, In Progress, On Hold, Completed
Changes task lifecycle state
Archive Task
Soft-archives tasks by setting archived = true
Archived tasks disappear from default filters, tiles, and views
