Design Guidance:

Edit Offer
As a Purchasing Agent
I want to edit offer details
So that I can correct errors or update information
Acceptance Criteria:
Trigger:
Click "Edit Offer" from actions menu
Opens edit form (inline or modal)
Edit Form:
Same layout as record form
All fields editable except: Offer ID, Created Date
Pre-filled with current values
Edit Rules by Status:
Received/Recorded/Recommended: Full edit access
Requested for Purchase: Cannot edit (locked)
Approved: Cannot edit (error message)
Rejected: Can edit
PO Created: Cannot edit
Save:
Validate required fields
Recalculate totals if pricing changed
Update "Last Modified" timestamp

 Duplicate Offer
As a Purchasing Agent
I want to duplicate an offer
So that I can create similar offers quickly
Acceptance Criteria:
Trigger:
Click "Duplicate Offer" from actions menu
Duplication:
Copy all fields except: 
Offer ID (generate new)
Status (set to "Draft")
Created Date (today)
Timeline (start fresh)
Copy attachments
Link to original: "Duplicated from [Offer ID]"
Immediate Edit:
Open duplicated offer in edit mode
Allow changes before saving
Show badge: "Duplicated from [ID]"
Save:
Creates new independent offer
Add to offers table

column4: Sourcing Request - Merging Multiple Sourcing Requests and handling exceeding requirement scenarios.
column6:
As a Purchasing Agent
I want to handle scenarios where offer quantity doesn't match request quantity
So that I can manage insufficient quantities appropriately

Acceptance criterion : 
If there are offers already received, and user is trying to add another sales request (which increases total qty) - attention popup: "Request quantity exceeds supplier offer, additional offer needed" 
Show current offers vs new total requirement
Option to request additional offers

Design guidance :

column4: Purchase order - Create Purchase Order from Multiple Approved Offers (Same Supplier)
column6:
As a Purchasing Manager
I want to create single PO from multiple offers
So that I consolidate orders

User selects multiple offers (checkboxes)
System validates: Must be same supplier
If different suppliers: Error message
Modal shows: 
All selected offers
Grand total
MANDATORY warehouse information collection during PO creation ← Dec 24 decision
On save: 
Creates ONE PO header
Creates multiple PO Lines (one per offer)
Auto-creates Stock Lines for each PO Line
Warehouse applies to all lines (inherited)

column4: Purchase order - Add to Existing Purchase Order (Same Supplier)
column6:
Summary 
As a buying agent 
I want to add to existing PO and add additional lines as needed as long as supplier is the same
So that I can manage multi-part orders efficiently

Acceptance Criteria:
METHOD A: From Approved Offer
User clicks "Purchase" from offer
Choice modal: "Create New PO" or "Add to Existing PO"
If "Add to Existing": 
Shows POs from same supplier only
Filters by status: Open, Awaiting Approval, Confirmed (excludes Closed/Canceled)
User selects PO
Validates: Supplier match (hard block if different)
If payment/shipping terms differ: Warning modal
Adds offer lines as new PO Lines (.04, .05, etc.)
Auto-creates Stock Lines for each new PO Line
Recalculates PO total
METHOD B: From PO Details (Manual Entry)
From PO details, click "Add Line"
If PO closed/canceled: Button disabled
Modal: Part#, mfr, qty, price, delivery date
Warehouse: Inherited from PO (read-only)
Optional: Link to SO
Creates PO Line + auto-creates Stock Line
Recalculates total

Summary 
As a Procurement Specialist
I want to update PO line status through the defined workflow
So that I can track progress from order to receipt

Status Flow until received: 
Awaiting Shipment → Shipped → Arrived → Tested → Received

Acceptance Criteria:
Given I have a PO line in any status
When I select "Update Status" from line actions menu
Then a modal appears showing: 
Current Status
Change to (dropdown with next available status)
Tracking Number (for Shipped status)
Quantity fields (Ordered, Shipped/Arrived based on transition)
And I can only progress to the next valid status in sequence
And I must provide required information for the transition
And when I submit, status updates and activity is logged
And aggregated parent PO status updates accordingly

column4: Purchase order - Auto Create stock line on PO creation
column6:
I want to automatically create stock lines when PO is created
So that inventory tracking begins immediately

Acceptance Criteria:
When PO is saved: 
For each PO Line created: 
System auto-creates one Stock Line
Stock Line fields: 
Quantity = PO Line quantity
Part (MFG, MPN) = from PO Line
Warehouse = from PO destination warehouse
Status = "Created"
PO Line reference
Allocation = inherited if sales-driven
Stock Line visible in: 
PO Line detail → Stock Lines section
Warehouse → Stock Lines list
User cannot manually delete this initial stock line
Stock line tracks: shipped_qty, arrived_qty (editable fields)
Default: 1 stock line per PO line (can split later during receiving)

column4: Purchase order - View aggregated status at parent PO
column6:
Summary: 
As a buying agent
I want to see aggregated status across all lines at the parent PO level
So that I can quickly understand overall PO progress
Acceptance Criteria:
Given I am viewing a PO with multiple lines (e.g., 5 parts / 49,000 units)
When I look at the PO header/summary
Then I see status breakdown: 
Shipped: 3 parts
Received: 3 parts
Tested: 0 parts
Allocated: 0 parts
And visual indicators showing progress
And overall PO status 
And aggregated status updates automatically when line statuses change

As a Purchasing Agent
I want to specify destination warehouse when creating PO
So that receiving team knows where to store inventory
Acceptance Criteria:
PO creation form includes "Destination Warehouse" field
Field type: Dropdown (required)
Options: Active warehouses only
Validation: Cannot save PO without warehouse selection
Error message: "Destination warehouse is required"
Warehouse cascades to: 
All PO Lines (inherit from PO)
All Stock Lines (auto-created with this warehouse)
Can change warehouse on PO Line level if different from PO default
Warehouse displayed on: 
PO header
PO Line cards
Stock Line records

column4: Purchase order - Update Arrival quantity on PO line
column6:
As a Warehouse Team Member
I want to record arrived quantity when shipment reaches warehouse
So that we track what's physically on-site

Acceptance Criteria:
On PO Line detail, "Shipping & Arrival" section
Field: Arrived Qty (numeric input, editable)
Available when: 
PO Line has shipped_qty > 0
Not yet started receiving
Validation: 
arrived_qty ≤ shipped_qty
arrived_qty ≥ 0
Can be less than shipped (partial arrival)
On update: 
Store arrived_qty on PO Line
Activity log: "Arrived quantity updated to {X}"
Status can be manually changed to "Arrived"
Timestamp logged
Parent PO counters recalculate
Can update multiple times (shipments arriving in batches)
Editable even after receiving starts (corrections allowed)

column4: Purchase order  - Start Receiving Process on PO Line
column6:
As a Warehouse Team Member
I want to start receiving process on PO Line
So that I can inspect shipment and split by lot/date codes

Acceptance Criteria:
On PO Line detail, click "Start Receiving" button
Button label: "Start Receiving" (renamed from "Split by Date Code or Lot #")
Available when: 
PO Line has arrived_qty > 0
Status = "Arrived" or "Shipped"
Not yet started receiving (can only start once)
Click action → Opens "Start Receiving" modal
Modal displays: 
PO Line summary: 
Part: {MFG} {MPN}
Ordered Qty: {X}
Shipped Qty: {Y}
Arrived Qty: {Z}
Instruction: "Physical inspection - Split by date code and lot number"
Create Stock Lines section:"+ Add Stock Line" button
Running total: "Created: 0 / Arrived: {Z}"
On "Start Receiving": 
PO Line status → "Receiving Started"
Timestamp logged
Activity log: "Receiving process started"
Modal stays open for creating stock lines

column4: Purchase order - Create additional Stock Lines During Receiving
column6:
As a Warehouse Team Member
I want to create stock lines for each unique lot/date code combination
So that each batch is tracked separately for traceability

Acceptance Criteria:
In "Start Receiving" modal, click "+ Add Stock Line"
Stock Line creation form: 
Quantity (required, numeric)
Lot Code (required, text)
Date Code Year (DCY) (required, 2 digits)
Date Code Week (DCW) (required, 2 digits)
Country of Origin (COO) (required, dropdown)
Warehouse (defaults to PO Line warehouse, can change)
Packaging Type: Reel / Tray / Tube / Box (optional)
Condition: Good / Damaged / Mixed (required)
Notes (optional, text area)
Running total updates: "Created: {sum} / Arrived: {arrived_qty}"
Validation: 
Each stock line must have unique combination of: Lot Code + Date Code + COO
Sum of all stock line quantities must equal arrived_qty
Cannot exceed arrived_qty
All required fields must be filled
Can add multiple stock lines (one per batch)
Can remove stock lines before submitting

column4: Purchase order - Complete Receiving and Create additional Stock Lines
column6:
As a Warehouse Team Member
I want to complete receiving process and create stock lines
So that inventory is ready for QC
Acceptance Criteria:
In "Start Receiving" modal, after creating all stock lines
Validation before submit: 
Sum of stock line quantities = arrived_qty
All required fields completed on each stock line
At least 1 stock line created
Click "Complete Receiving" button
On submit: 
Create all Stock Lines in database
Each Stock Line created with: 
Status = "Received" (ready for QC)
Quantity = as specified
Lot Code, Date Code, COO as specified
Warehouse location
Link to parent PO Line
Allocation inherited if sales-driven
Delete original auto-created stock line (the placeholder)
PO Line status → "Receiving Complete"
Activity log on PO Line: "{N} stock lines created during receiving"
Activity log on each Stock Line: "Created during receiving process"
Timestamp logged
Parent PO status recalculates
Stock Lines now visible in: 
PO Line → Stock Lines section
Warehouse → Stock Lines list
QC queue (status = "Received" means "Ready for QC")

column4: Purchase order - Handling shortages and overages
Purchase order - QC - Stock line details & Assign QC Controller to Stock Line
Purchase order - Handle QC Fail - Initiate Return (RMA)

column6:
As a Purchasing Agent
I want to link sales orders to purchase orders and manage allocation
So that I can track fulfillment and reserve inventory for customers

Acceptance Criteria:
Link Sales Orders:
 View table of linked sales orders with: SO ID, customer, part, quantity, value, status
 Add sales orders via search modal
 Select specific SO lines to link
 Specify allocated quantity per SO line
 Display "Allocated" quantity for each linked SO
Allocation Management:
 Show allocation details: warehouse location, order delivery period
 View allocation table: SO line, customer, quantity ordered, quantity allocated
 Edit allocation amounts (cannot exceed available or SO quantity)
 Track total allocated vs available
 Link multiple SOs if quantities allow
Status Tracking:
 SO status reflects PO line status
 Click SO ID to navigate to sales order
 Three-dot menu: Edit allocation, Remove link
Business Rules:
Sales orders must have matching part number to PO lines
Cannot allocate more than available PO quantity or SO quantity
For sales-driven POs: Pre-allocated at creation (1:1 locked)
For proactive POs: Post-allocated after receiving (1:many flexible)
Allocation only available after status = Received

Summary 
As a Purchasing Agent
I want to view and edit all purchase order information
So that I can maintain accurate procurement records

Acceptance Criteria:
PO Overview:
 Display PO header: ID, order date, supplier PO#, parts count, total costs, amount due
 Show status summary with progress indicators: Shipped, Arrived, Tested, Received
 Display supplier information: Account name, primary contact, location
 Show active contact with phone/email (clickable)
Supplier & Contact Management:
 View and edit primary contact from dropdown
 Add new contacts to supplier account
 Update account owner and salesperson
 Save/cancel changes with validation
Shipping Information:
 Select delivery warehouse from dropdown (auto-fills address)
 Edit address fields: street, city, state, zip
 Specify INCO terms and ship via method
 Set earliest ship date with "ship together" option
 Editable only before PO is shipped
Costs & Invoicing:
 View line-item cost breakdown (part costs subtotal)
 Add freight costs and miscellaneous costs
 Auto-calculate total invoice: Part + Freight + Misc costs
 Display billing contact, address, payment terms, tax rate
 Edit billing information and payment terms
References & Tags:
 Display internal IDs (Chip1 SO ID) and external IDs (Supplier PO ID)
 Add custom IDs for external system references (max 10)
 Add and manage tags for categorization (max 20)
 Tags enable filtering and reporting

column4: Sales/Purchasing workflow changes - Offers on Sourcing request
column6:
Summary : 
As a purchasing team member
I want to add offers to a sourcing request
So that the sales team can see available options and pricing

Acceptance Criteria:
Status updates to "Offers Received" when offers are added
Status updates to "Offers Gathered" when purchasing marks gathering complete
Introduce a sub-status "Target price achieved" to track when an offer meets target price 
Same should be passed onto UI so they consume and show at relevant sections like list views, status summary, chevrons etc

column4: Sales/Purchasing workflow changes - Quote creation from offers and quote creation without offers - Advancement into SO
column6:
Summary : 
As a salesperson
I want to create a quote from a specific offer
So that the system knows which offer they are using for pricing

Acceptance Criteria:
Quote can be created from an offer when the target price is met on offers 
Connection between quote and offer is established
that way quote always has the reference of Sourcing Request and Offer

If the quote is created without the offer but from RFQ 
Quote can still be created without the offer connection 
In such a case when SO is created from such quotes, sourcing request references have to be picked from RFQ 
Sales Order shows both sourcing request and offer

column4: Sales/Purchasing workflow changes - Request for purchase from Sales Order
column6:
Summary 
As a sales team agent
I want to create a sourcing request from Sales Order when one doesn't exist and copied from RFQ
So that I can request purchasing even if sales agent quoted without engaging purchasing initially

Acceptance Criteria:
Sourcing request can be created from Sales Order
New sourcing request can connect to existing sourcing request for same part
sourcing request status is directly set to "Request for purchase" (Advice - Not set directly to Request for purchase but have an option given to the user to choose the status)
Sales Agent can leave notes about market price and available sources

Display Supplier Contact Information
Show supplier contact details (name, phone, email) in sourcing request view in an island and as well as on the offer detail page
2. Quantity Alignment per Supplier
Allow editing requested quantity per supplier when creating offer requests
Default to sourcing request quantity with ability to adjust individually
Show historical MOQ data to guide quantity decisions
Support bulk actions: "Set All to Same", "Set to MOQ", "Distribute Evenly"
Each supplier receives their specific quantity in email/system
3. Email Integration for Offer Requests
Automatically send professional RFQ emails to suppliers when offer requests are created
Email includes: part details, quantity, target price (optional), delivery date, specifications
Track email delivery status: sent, delivered, opened, bounced
Support resend functionality for failed deliveries
Log all email activity in offer timeline
4. "No Longer Available" Status
Add quick action to mark offers as unavailable when supplier cannot fulfill
Require reason selection: out of stock, discontinued, supplier withdrew, etc.
Preserve historical data (don't delete), set display quantity to 0
Update sourcing request metrics automatically
Optional supplier notification email
Support restore/undo by authorized users
5. Standardized Status Lifecycle
Implement clear status progression with defined transitions:
Primary Statuses:
Requested - Offer requested, awaiting response
Received - Supplier provided quote
Recorded - Manually entered by buyer
Under Review - Being evaluated
Recommended - Selected for approval
Approved - Management approved
PO Created - Purchase order generated
Rejected - Not selected
No Longer Available - Supplier cannot fulfill
Expired - Quote validity passed
Sub-statuses: Optional additional context (e.g., "Received (Complete Response)", "Rejected (Higher Price)")
Status Rules:
Color coding: Blue (Requested), Green (Received/Recorded), Yellow (Under Review/Recommended), Dark Green (Approved/PO Created), Red (Rejected/Unavailable), Gray (Expired)
Allowed transitions defined (e.g., cannot go from PO Created back to Under Review)
Status changes logged with timestamp and user
Trigger appropriate actions and notifications per status
6. Tags for Special Offers
Support tagging offers to highlight special conditions, visible in list view:
System-Generated Tags (Auto-applied):
#AggressiveOffer - Price significantly below target (>15% discount)
#BestPrice - Lowest price among all offers
#FastDelivery - Lead time < 7 days
#PreferredSupplier - From preferred supplier list
#ExpiringOffer - Quote expires within 48 hours
Manual Tags: Users can add custom tags (#HighQuality, #NewSupplier, etc.)
Tag Display:
Show as colored pills in offer list "Tags" column
Display max 3 tags, "+X more" if exceeds
Click tag to filter offers
Tags included in search and filtering options
Alert notifications for #AggressiveOffer and #ExpiringOffer

column4: Offer matching - Adding match indicator to Independent offers
column6:
As a Purchasing Team Member
I want to see which market offers have matching demand (RFQs or Sales Orders)
So that I can prioritize offers that align with current customer needs

Acceptance Criteria:
Each offer record includes a boolean has_match flag
Flag is stored in the offer database table
Flag defaults to false when offer is created
Flag can be updated by the system based on matching logic

As a system
I want to automatically check for matches when a new offer is created
So that the match flag is set correctly from the start
Acceptance Criteria:
When a new offer is created, system automatically checks for matching RFQs
When a new offer is created, system automatically checks for matching Sales Orders
Match is identified when offer MPN and Manufacturer match RFQ/SO line items
Only consider RFQs that are NOT in "Closed" status
Only consider Sales Orders that are NOT in "Procured" status
Set has_match = true if ANY matching RFQ or SO is found
Set has_match = false if NO matches found
Matching logic executes synchronously during offer creation
Business Rules:
Match criteria: Exact match on MPN AND Manufacturer
RFQ status exclusion: Ignore RFQs with status = "Closed"
Sales Order status exclusion: Ignore SOs with status = "" ??

column4: Offer matching - Auto update related to RFQ and SO changes
column6:
As a system
I want to automatically update offer match flags when RFQs or Sales Orders are created or updated
So that match indicators stay current as demand changes
Acceptance Criteria:
When RFQ is created:
System identifies all existing offers with matching MPN and Manufacturer
Updates has_match = true for matched offers
Only if RFQ status is NOT "Closed"
When RFQ is updated:
System re-evaluates matches with existing offers
Updates match flags based on current RFQ status and part details
If RFQ status changes to "Closed", remove matches (set has_match = false if no other matches exist)
When Sales Order is created:
System identifies all existing offers with matching MPN and Manufacturer
Updates has_match = true for matched offers
Only if SO status is NOT "Procured"
When Sales Order is updated:
System re-evaluates matches with existing offers
Updates match flags based on current SO status and part details
If SO status changes to "Procured", remove matches (set has_match = false if no other matches exist)
Business Rules:
Match evaluation continues until RFQ reaches "Closed" status
Match evaluation continues until Sales Order reaches "Procured" status
When RFQ/SO reaches terminal status, only remove match flag if no OTHER active RFQs/SOs match
Match criteria remains: Exact match on MPN AND Manufacturer
